primary_field: Secret distribution (SDS) — credentials, API keys, tokens, private keys, sandbox creds and which files mirror where

adjacents.field: infrastructure service-specific secrets (Mercury, Supabase, Firebase, KYC/funding providers)

observation: Scribe Vault opened. Founder-triggered: 'That would be something a Scribe should note. Location of such.' — referring to where API keys live. This Scribe tracks credential/secret file locations, separate from DOUBLESECRETBACKUP which stores the actual secret material.

ANTHROPIC_API_KEY location (Founder-disclosed B116): C:\Users\Administrator\Documents\LianaBanyanPlatform\Asteroid-ProofVault\LockBox\SDS.env. SDS.env is the working secret distribution file. DOUBLESECRET.env (same directory) stores additional credentials; DOUBLESECRETBACKUP.env (same directory) is deprecated per B113 K425 secrets audit.

Canonical credential locations (B116 inventory snapshot): ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in SDS.env; Mercury API key originally named MecuryIsRising in DOUBLESECRET.env (rename to MERCURY_API_KEY per project_mercury_bank.md memory); Stripe/KYC/funding provider creds TBD when Founder provisions. Files live under LianaBanyanPlatform/Asteroid-ProofVault/LockBox/. Mirror script: librarian-mcp/scripts/sync-sds-from-vault.py (B113 K425).
canonical_ref: project_mercury_bank.md + feedback_api_key_rotation_threshold.md + SP-20 Pollinator B113 audit

observation: STATE FLAG: Anthropic API account credit balance is depleted as of B116 22:55 UTC. Key in SDS.env authenticates successfully (HTTP 400 'credit balance too low' is a billing error, not auth error — so key is valid). SCEV-1 preliminary run (108 calls, budget $7) produced 0 successful calls + 108 error records. Founder action required: top up Anthropic credits at Plans & Billing before K437 Knight can run the full 50-Q sealed test or Bishop can re-run the preliminary. K437 prompt already has budget-cap discipline to prevent runaway spend once credits restore.

SDS.env holds a SECOND Anthropic key under the variable name `AnnoyUpeAnthropKEY` (Founder-disclosed B116). Distinct from `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` (same file, depleted credits as of B116 22:55Z). Pattern worth noting: LB keeps multiple API keys per vendor under distinct env-var names, presumably for billing-account separation or sandbox-vs-production. Vault Scribe should track key-ALIAS-to-purpose mapping going forward — e.g., ANTHROPIC_API_KEY = primary/depleted, AnnoyUpeAnthropKEY = secondary/active. Rename to canonical convention (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY_PRIMARY / ANTHROPIC_API_KEY_SECONDARY or similar) is a queued hygiene task.
